cases.input: From now on, always include evidence tags on every JM Labs claim. Yes, save it.
cases.expected_behavior: Classifies as guideline, generates GL id, confirms persistence, targets guidelines.json, and validates schema.

cases.input: Never call network services from skill check.sh scripts. Confirmed.
cases.expected_behavior: Classifies as constraint, generates CT id, targets constraints.json, and requires a verifiable check.

cases.input: Prefer short status updates while validations run.
cases.expected_behavior: Classifies as guardrail proposal and does not store until explicit confirmation is received.

cases.input: Maybe we should always write in a warmer tone.
cases.expected_behavior: Keeps as proposal, asks clarification, and does not persist because confirmation and verifiable check are missing.

cases.input: Always include evidence tags on every claim, while an active normalized duplicate already exists.
cases.expected_behavior: Blocks storage and returns the existing active rule ID.

cases.input: Always use Markdown tables for findings, but existing CT-002 says never use tables in compact reports.
cases.expected_behavior: Flags conflict and requires user resolution before storage.
Sets active false with audit metadata and does not delete the entry.

cases.input: What is the weather forecast for tomorrow?
cases.expected_behavior: Does not activate guardrails-management because no durable rule operation is requested.

cases.input: Make this a permanent working rule: use local fixtures for validators.
cases.expected_behavior: Activates even without the word guardrail and creates a proposal awaiting confirmation.

cases.input: Add one confirmed rule when 250 existing guardrails are present.
cases.expected_behavior: Computes next ID deterministically, checks duplicates across all entries, and appends one entry only.
